author: doll-agent
content:
AVX-512 DGEMM kernel translated from OpenBLAS dgemm_kernel_16x2_skylakex.c, gated behind opt-in cargo feature `avx512`.

Files added/modified:
- ferray-linalg/src/gemm/kernel_avx512.rs — kernel_4x16_avx512 (4 rows x 16 cols register tile, 8 zmm accumulators of 8 doubles each) + kernel_edge_avx512. Per K iter: 2 zmm B-loads (16 doubles), 4 broadcasts of A (4 doubles), 8 FMAs = 128 FLOPs / 4 cycles = 32 FLOPs/cycle = AVX-512 DGEMM peak (twice the AVX2 peak from doubled lane count).
- ferray-linalg/src/gemm/pack.rs — pack_b_f64_nr16 (B-pack with NR=16, sibling of pack_b which uses NR=8). Reuses the f64 pack_a since MR_AVX512 == MR_AVX2 == 4.
- ferray-linalg/src/gemm/outer.rs — gemm_avx512_f64 (parallel M-slab + 2D grid using pick_grid) + gemm_seq_avx512_f64 (sequential 5-loop). Reuses A_PACK_TLS, adds B_PACK_TLS_AVX512 for the wider strip.
- ferray-linalg/src/gemm/mod.rs — cpu_supports_avx512f() + gemm_f64() routes to AVX-512 when the feature is on AND the CPU detects avx512f.
- ferray-linalg/Cargo.toml — feature `avx512` (off by default; enabling bumps effective MSRV to 1.89 because std::arch::x86_64 AVX-512 intrinsics stabilised in Rust 1.89).

Layout choice: OpenBLAS's reference kernel uses a 16-rows x 12-cols column-oriented register tile (24 zmm accumulators) with vunpcklpd/vunpckhpd transposes at SAVE time for column-major C output. Our row-major contract makes the row-oriented 4x16 layout (parallel to the AVX2 4x8 design) cleaner — each zmm holds one row's 8 contiguous doubles, two zmm cover one tile row, fast vmovups stores with no transpose.

Test status: kernel_4x16_avx512_matches_naive and kernel_4x16_avx512_beta_one_accumulates skip cleanly on AVX2-only CPUs (i7-14700K) via is_x86_feature_detected!("avx512f") guard. Both build + test cleanly with --features avx512:
- cargo test -p ferray-linalg --release: 210 unit + 21 oracle + 12 property = 243 tests pass
- cargo test -p ferray-linalg --release --features avx512: 212 unit + 21 + 12 = 245 tests pass (adds 2 AVX-512 kernel tests, both skip on this CPU)

Cannot benchmark on the development machine (Raptor Lake i7-14700K has no AVX-512). Users with Sapphire Rapids, Skylake-X, Cascade Lake, Ice Lake server, or Zen4 CPUs should enable `--features avx512` to get the f64 multi-thread bump. Expected performance: roughly 2x DGEMM throughput vs the AVX2 path (matches the doubled lane count, mirrored by the SGEMM port's measured 2.2x speedup vs DGEMM at the same problem size on the AVX2 path).

Combined with #660 SGEMM port:
- **Default build (any Rust >=1.85)**: AVX2 DGEMM (translated 4x8) + AVX2 SGEMM (translated 4x16) on x86_64
- **--features avx512 (Rust >=1.89)**: AVX-512 DGEMM (translated 4x16) added; runtime CPU detection picks AVX-512 path when avx512f present, falls through to AVX2 when not.
